# dragonfly-radiance

Dragonfly extension for Radiance simulation.

## Installation

`pip install dragonfly-radiance`

To check if the command line interface is installed correctly
use `dragonfly-radiance --help`.

## QuickStart

```python
import dragonfly_radiance
```

## [API Documentation](http://ladybug-tools.github.io/dragonfly-radiance/docs)

## Local Development

1. Clone this repo locally
```
git clone git@github.com:ladybug-tools/dragonfly-radiance

# or

git clone https://github.com/ladybug-tools/dragonfly-radiance
```
2. Install dependencies:
```
cd dragonfly-radiance
pip install -r dev-requirements.txt
pip install -r requirements.txt
```

3. Run Tests:
```
python -m pytest tests/
```

4. Generate Documentation:
```
sphinx-apidoc -f -e -d 4 -o ./docs ./dragonfly_radiance
sphinx-build -b html ./docs ./docs/_build/docs
```

functions.name: dragonfly_radiance.cli.translate.model_to_rad
documentation:
Translate a Dragonfly Model file to a Radiance string.

The resulting string will include all geometry and all modifiers.

 
Args:
    model_file: Full path to a Dragonfly Model JSON or Pkl file.

documentation: Initialize Building radiance properties.

documentation: Get the Building object hosting these properties.

documentation:
Get or set the Building ModifierSet object.

If not set, it will be the Honeybee default generic ModifierSet.

methods.name: dragonfly_radiance.properties.building.BuildingRadianceProperties.add_grid_parameter
documentation:
Add a GridParameter to all of the children Room2Ds of this Building.

Args:
    grid_par: A radiance GridParameter object to assign to all children Room2Ds.

methods.name: dragonfly_radiance.properties.building.BuildingRadianceProperties.from_dict
documentation:
Create BuildingRadianceProperties from a dictionary.

Note that the dictionary must be a non-abridged version for this
classmethod to work.

Args:
    data: A dictionary representation of BuildingRadianceProperties.
    host: A Building object that hosts these properties.

methods.name: dragonfly_radiance.properties.building.BuildingRadianceProperties.apply_properties_from_dict
documentation:
Apply properties from a BuildingRadiancePropertiesAbridged dictionary.

Args:
    abridged_data: A BuildingRadiancePropertiesAbridged dictionary (typically
        coming from a Model).
    modifier_sets: A dictionary of ModifierSets with identifiers
        of the sets as keys, which will be used to re-assign modifier_sets.

methods.name: dragonfly_radiance.properties.building.BuildingRadianceProperties.apply_properties_from_geojson_dict
documentation:
Apply properties from a geoJSON dictionary.

Args:
    data: A dictionary representation of a geoJSON feature properties.
        Specifically, this should be the "properties" key describing
        a Polygon or MultiPolygon object.

methods.name: dragonfly_radiance.properties.building.BuildingRadianceProperties.to_dict
documentation:
Return Building Radiance properties as a dictionary.

Args:
    abridged: Boolean for whether the full dictionary of the Building should
        be written (False) or just the identifier of the the individual
        properties (True). Default: False.

methods.name: dragonfly_radiance.properties.building.BuildingRadianceProperties.duplicate
documentation:
Get a copy of this object.

new_host: A new Building object that hosts these properties.
    If None, the properties will be duplicated with the same host.

Radiance Properties for Dragonfly Building.

Args:
    host: A dragonfly_core Building object that hosts these properties.
    modifier_set: A honeybee ModifierSet object to specify all default modifiers
        for the Story geometry. If None, it will be the honeybee default modifier
        set, which is only representative of typical indoor conditions in
        the visible spectrum. (Default: None).

Properties:
    * host
    * modifier_set

Args:

methods.name: dragonfly_radiance.properties.model.ModelRadianceProperties.modifiers
documentation:
List of all unique modifiers contained within the model.

This includes modifiers across all Room2Ds, Stories, and Building
ModifierSets but it does NOT include the Honeybee generic default
modifier set.

methods.name: dragonfly_radiance.properties.model.ModelRadianceProperties.face_modifiers
documentation:
Get a list of all unique modifiers assigned to Faces, Apertures and Doors.

These objects only exist under the Building.room_3ds property.

methods.name: dragonfly_radiance.properties.model.ModelRadianceProperties.shade_modifiers
documentation: A list of all unique modifiers assigned to ContextShades in the model.

methods.name: dragonfly_radiance.properties.model.ModelRadianceProperties.modifier_sets
documentation:
A list of all unique Building-Assigned ModifierSets in the Model.

Note that this includes ModifierSets assigned to individual Stories and
Room2Ds in the Building.

methods.name: dragonfly_radiance.properties.model.ModelRadianceProperties.global_modifier_set
documentation:
The global radiance modifier set.

This is what is used whenever there is no modifier_set assigned to a
Room2D, a parent Story, or a parent Building.

methods.name: dragonfly_radiance.properties.model.ModelRadianceProperties.check_all
documentation:
Check all of the aspects of the Model radiance properties.

Args:
    raise_exception: Boolean to note whether a ValueError should be raised
        if any errors are found. If False, this method will simply
        return a text string with all errors that were found.

Returns:
    A text string with all errors that were found. This string will be empty
    of no errors were found.

methods.name: dragonfly_radiance.properties.model.ModelRadianceProperties.check_duplicate_modifier_set_identifiers
documentation: Check that there are no duplicate ModifierSet identifiers in the model.

methods.name: dragonfly_radiance.properties.model.ModelRadianceProperties.apply_properties_from_dict
documentation:
Apply the radiance properties of a dictionary to the host Model of this object.

Args:
    data: A dictionary representation of an entire dragonfly-core Model.
        Note that this dictionary must have ModelRadianceProperties in order
        for this method to successfully apply the radiance properties.

documentation: Return Model radiance properties as a dictionary.

documentation: Check if a modifier is assigned to an object and add it to a list.

documentation: Check if a modifier set is assigned to an object and add it to a list.

methods.name: dragonfly_radiance.properties.model.ModelRadianceProperties._instance_in_array
documentation:
Check if a specific object instance is already in an array.

This can be much faster than  `if object_instance in object_array`
when you expect to be testing a lot of the same instance of an object for
inclusion in an array since the builtin method uses an == operator to
test inclusion.

Radiance Properties for Dragonfly Model.

Args:
    host: A dragonfly_core Model object that hosts these properties.

Properties:
    * host
    * modifiers
    * shade_modifiers
    * modifier_sets
    * global_modifier_set

  host: A Room2D object that hosts these properties.

```python
"grid_parameters": []  # A list of GridParameter dictionaries
```

    modifier_set: A honeybee ModifierSet object to specify all default
        modifiers for the Faces of the Room2D. If None, the Room2D will use
        the honeybee default modifier set, which is only representative
        of typical indoor conditions in the visible spectrum. (Default: None).
    grid_parameters: An optional list of GridParameter objects to describe
        how sensor grids should be generated for the Room2D. (Default: None).

Properties:
    * host
    * modifier_set
    * grid_parameters

methods.name: dragonfly_radiance.gridpar._GridParameterBase.duplicate
documentation: Get a copy of this object.

Base object for all GridParameters.

This object records all of the methods that must be overwritten on a grid
parameter object for it to be successfully be applied in dragonfly workflows.

Args:
    dimension: The dimension of the grid cells as a number.
    offset: A number for how far to offset the grid from the base
        geometries. (Default: 0).
    include_mesh: A boolean to note whether the resulting SensorGrid should
        include the mesh. (Default: True).

methods.name: dragonfly_radiance.gridpar.RoomGridParameter.duplicate
documentation: Get a copy of this object.

Instructions for a SensorGrid generated from a Room2D's floors.

The resulting grid will have the room referenced in its room_identifier
property. Note that the grid is generated within the XY coordinate system
of the Room2D's floor_geometry. So rotating the plane of this geometry will
will result in rotated grid cells.

Args:
    dimension: The dimension of the grid cells as a number.
    offset: A number for how far to offset the grid from the base
        geometries. (Default: 1, suitable for Rooms in Meters).
    wall_offset: A number for the distance at which sensors close to walls
        should be removed. Note that this option has no effect unless the
        value is more than half of the dimension. (Default: 0).
    include_mesh: A boolean to note whether the resulting SensorGrid should
        include the mesh. (Default: True).

classes.name: dragonfly_radiance.gridpar.RoomRadialGridParameter

methods.name: dragonfly_radiance.gridpar.RoomRadialGridParameter.dir_count
documentation: Get an integer for the number of radial directions around each position.

methods.name: dragonfly_radiance.gridpar.RoomRadialGridParameter.start_vector
documentation: Get a Vector3D that sets the start direction of the generated directions.

methods.name: dragonfly_radiance.gridpar.RoomRadialGridParameter.mesh_radius
documentation:
Get a number that sets the radius of the meshes generated around each sensor.

If None or autocalculate, it will be equal to 45% of the grid dimension.

methods.name: dragonfly_radiance.gridpar.RoomRadialGridParameter.generate_grid_from_room
documentation:
Get a SensorGrid from a Honeybee Room using these GridParameter.

Args:
    honeybee_room: A Honeybee Room to which these grid parameters are applied.

Returns:
    A honeybee-radiance SensorGrid generated from the Honeybee Room. Will
    be None if a valid Grid cannot be generated from the Room.

methods.name: dragonfly_radiance.gridpar.RoomRadialGridParameter.scale
documentation:
Get a scaled version of these GridParameters.

This method is called within the scale methods of the Room2D.

Args:
    factor: A number representing how much the object should be scaled.

methods.name: dragonfly_radiance.gridpar.RoomRadialGridParameter.from_dict
documentation:
Create RoomRadialGridParameter from a dictionary.

```python
{
"type": "RoomRadialGridParameter",
"dimension": 1.0,
"offset": 1.2,
"wall_offset": 1.0,
"dir_count": 8,
"start_vector": (0, -1, 0),
"mesh_radius": 0.5,
"include_mesh": True
}
```

methods.name: dragonfly_radiance.gridpar.RoomRadialGridParameter.to_dict
documentation: Get RoomRadialGridParameter as a dictionary.

methods.name: dragonfly_radiance.gridpar.RoomRadialGridParameter.duplicate
documentation: Get a copy of this object.

Instructions for a SensorGrid of radial directions around positions from floors.

This type of sensor grid is particularly helpful for studies of multiple
view directions, such as imageless glare studies.

The resulting grid will have the room referenced in its room_identifier
property. Note that the grid is generated within the XY coordinate system
of the Room2D's floor_geometry. So rotating the plane of this geometry will
will result in rotated grid cells.

Args:
    dimension: The dimension of the grid cells as a number.
    offset: A number for how far to offset the grid from the base
        geometries. (Default: 1.2, suitable for Rooms in Meters).
    wall_offset: A number for the distance at which sensors close to walls
        should be removed. Note that this option has no effect unless the
        value is more than half of the x_dim or y_dim. (Default: 0).
    dir_count: A positive integer for the number of radial directions
        to be generated around each position. (Default: 8).
    start_vector: A Vector3D to set the start direction of the generated
        directions. This can be used to orient the resulting sensors to
        specific parts of the scene. It can also change the elevation of the
        resulting directions since this start vector will always be rotated in
        the XY plane to generate the resulting directions. (Default: (0, -1, 0)).
    mesh_radius: An optional number to override the radius of the meshes
        generated around each sensor. If None or autocalculate, it will be
        equal to 45% of the grid dimension. (Default: None).
    include_mesh: A boolean to note whether the resulting SensorGrid should
        include the mesh. (Default: True).

```python
}
```

methods.name: dragonfly_radiance.gridpar.ExteriorFaceGridParameter.to_dict
documentation: Get ExteriorFaceGridParameter as a dictionary.

methods.name: dragonfly_radiance.gridpar.ExteriorFaceGridParameter.duplicate
documentation: Get a copy of this object.

Instructions for a SensorGrid generated from exterior Faces.

Args:
    dimension: The dimension of the grid cells as a number.
    offset: A number for how far to offset the grid from the base
        geometries. (Default: 0.1, suitable for Rooms in Meters).
    face_type: Text to specify the type of face that will be used to
        generate grids. Note that only Faces with Outdoors boundary
        conditions will be used, meaning that most Floors will typically
        be excluded unless they represent the underside of a cantilever.
        Choose from the following. (Default: Wall).

        * Wall
        * Roof
        * Floor
        * All

    punched_geometry: Boolean to note whether the punched_geometry of the faces
        should be used (True) with the areas of sub-faces removed from the grid
        or the full geometry should be used (False). (Default:False).
    include_mesh: A boolean to note whether the resulting SensorGrid should
        include the mesh. (Default: True).

classes.name: dragonfly_radiance.gridpar.ExteriorApertureGridParameter

methods.name: dragonfly_radiance.gridpar.ExteriorApertureGridParameter.aperture_type
documentation: Get text to specify the type of face that will be used to generate grids.

methods.name: dragonfly_radiance.gridpar.ExteriorApertureGridParameter.generate_grid_from_room
documentation:
Get a SensorGrid from a Honeybee Room using these GridParameter.

Args:
    honeybee_room: A Honeybee Room to which these grid parameters are applied.

Returns:
    A honeybee-radiance SensorGrid generated from the Honeybee Room. Will
    be None if the object has no exterior Apertures.

methods.name: dragonfly_radiance.gridpar.ExteriorApertureGridParameter.scale
documentation:
Get a scaled version of these GridParameters.

This method is called within the scale methods of the Room2D.

Args:
    factor: A number representing how much the object should be scaled.

methods.name: dragonfly_radiance.gridpar.ExteriorApertureGridParameter.from_dict
documentation:
Create ExteriorApertureGridParameter from a dictionary.

```python
{
"type": "ExteriorApertureGridParameter",
"dimension": 0.5,
"offset": 0.15,
"aperture_type": "Window",
"include_mesh": True
}
```

methods.name: dragonfly_radiance.gridpar.ExteriorApertureGridParameter.to_dict
documentation: Get ExteriorApertureGridParameter as a dictionary.

methods.name: dragonfly_radiance.gridpar.ExteriorApertureGridParameter.duplicate
documentation: Get a copy of this object.

Instructions for a SensorGrid generated from exterior Aperture.

Args:
    dimension: The dimension of the grid cells as a number.
    offset: A number for how far to offset the grid from the base
        geometries. (Default: 0.1, suitable for Rooms in Meters).
    aperture_type: Text to specify the type of Aperture that will be used to
        generate grids. Window indicates Apertures in Walls. Skylights
        are in parent Roof faces. Choose from the following. (Default: All).

        * Window
        * Skylight
        * All

    include_mesh: A boolean to note whether the resulting SensorGrid should
        include the mesh. (Default: True).
